protocol.name: kde_overlay_shell_v1

description.summary: Overlay on top of other windows

protocol.description:
This protocol allows the client to create "overlay" surfaces that are on top of xdg_toplevel windows of other clients.
The key words "must", "must not", "required", "shall", "shall not", "should", "should not", "recommended", "may", and "optional" in this document are to be interpreted as described in IETF RFC 2119.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

interface.name: kde_overlay_shell_manager_v1
interface.description: This interface is used to create overlay surfaces.

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy the overlay shell manager object

request.description: Destroys the manager object. Objects created by this manager are not affected.

request.name: get_overlay_surface
request.description:
Creates an overlay surface attached to the toplevel described by the toplevel handle.
If the toplevel still exists when the request is processed, the compositor will respond with a configure event on the overlay surface.
If the toplevel doesn't exist when this request is processed, the compositor will immediately send the kde_overlay_surface_v1.closed event on the newly created object.

entry.name: role
entry.summary: given wl_surface already has a role

interface.name: kde_overlay_surface_v1
interface.description:
When this object is created, the compositor immediately sends either the closed event, or a configure event. The client must wait for the first configure event and ack it before it may attach the first buffer.
The client may attach a null buffer to the surface at any time to hide the overlay, and commit a non-null buffer again to show it again. TODO add a request_configure for this case, so it definitely matches the latest desired size?

request.name: destroy
request.description: This informs the compositor that the lock surface object will no longer be used.

event.name: closed
event.description: The window this overlay was attached to was closed, and the overlay is no longer shown. The client should

entry.name: commit_before_first_ack
entry.summary: surface committed before first ack_configure request

entry.name: dimensions_mismatch
entry.summary: failed to match width or height of configure event

entry.name: invalid_serial
entry.summary: serial provided in ack_configure is invalid

request.name: ack_configure
request.description:
When a configure event is received, once the client commits the surface in response, the client must make an ack_configure request before the commit request, passing along the serial of the configure event.
If the client receives multiple configure events before it can respond to one, it only has to ack the last configure event.
A client is not required to commit immediately after sending an ack_configure request - it may even ack_configure several times before its next surface commit.
A client may send multiple ack_configure requests before committing, but only the last request sent before a commit indicates which configure event the client really is responding to.
Sending an ack_configure request consumes the configure event referenced by the given serial, as well as all older configure events sent on this object.
It is a protocol error to issue multiple ack_configure requests referencing the same configure event or to issue an ack_configure request referencing a configure event older than the last configure event acked for a given lock surface.

event.name: configure

description.summary: the client should resize its surface

event.description:
This event is sent once on binding the interface and may be sent again at the compositor's discretion, for example if the overlayed window is resized. The client should respond as soon as possible with the ack_configure request and matching commit.
The width and height are in surface-local coordinates and are exact requirements. Failing to match these surface dimensions in the next commit after acking a configure is a protocol error.
